gamePitch: an iyashikei (healing slice-of-life) collectible TCG set across four tranquil countryside wards — forest, coast, hearth, and highland

world.title: The Four Quiet Wards
world.description: Beyond the hurried cities lies a chain of pastoral wards where everyday moments hold quiet power. Collect spirits of the countryside, build gentle-yet-tactical decks, and compete without losing the calm that defines Komorebi.

shinrin.visualIdentity: cedar forests, moss shrines, tea terraces, kodama spirits, soft komorebi light, sage and cedar brown palette
shinrin.flavorNotes: guardians, druids, kodama keepers, moss spirits, rootbound healers, shrine tenders

naminami.visualIdentity: calm inlets, fishing piers, tidal gardens, sea mist, herons and tide listeners, pale blue and shell-white tones
naminami.flavorNotes: tide listeners, pearl divers, heron spirits, coral whispers, wave shamans, harbor guides

akari.visualIdentity: golden hour hills, harvest orchards, festival lanterns, onsen steam, warm amber and copper palette
akari.flavorNotes: lantern tenders, festival dancers, embersmiths, hearth cooks, orchard keepers, sunset weavers

takane.visualIdentity: highland windmills, cloud pastures, wind chimes, open sky, lavender and meadow-green palette
takane.flavorNotes: breeze shepherds, cloud readers, summit guides, wind chime monks, meadow spirits, kite flyers

designRules: Mana 1–8 (most cards 3–6). Spirit + Calm should roughly scale with mana (stored as attack and health in JSON).
Roles vary: guardians, scouts, healers, listeners, drifters, tenders — not assassins, siege units, or burn decks.
Ability text must spell out keyword effects like the examples (do not assume the player knows keywords).
ability.name should be evocative and unique within the batch.
Use 0–2 keywords per card from the project glossary only.
Tone: gentle iyashikei flavor — pastoral, nostalgic, healing — still tactically interesting but never brutal.
Never use violent language in ability text: no damage, kill, destroy, burn, siege, or direct attacks on the player.
Frame contests as easing composure, tending Calm, redirecting engagement, or drawing/gathering — not dealing damage.
Rarity tiers (from game/rarities.json): Humble, Gentle, Radiant, Sacred, Komorebi — derived from mana unless rarity is set explicitly.

image.promptGuidelines: single focal spirit or countryside character, soft iyashikei anime trading card art, watercolor-influenced, gentle natural lighting, NO text, NO card frame, NO watermark, portrait 3:4 composition
image.promptSuffix: Soft iyashikei anime trading card art, watercolor-influenced illustration, single focal subject, gentle natural lighting, pastoral mood, no text, no border, no watermark, portrait 3:4 aspect ratio.
image.notesGuidelines: negative prompts, palette, time of day, and pastoral mood hints for the image generator

rarities.notes: Tier ids (common, uncommon, …) match the DB enum. Display labels: Humble, Gentle, Radiant, Sacred, Komorebi.
